roles.title: General Dentist
roles.overview: A General Dentist provides comprehensive dental care for patients of all ages. They diagnose, treat, and prevent oral health issues such as tooth decay, gum disease, and infections while promoting overall oral hygiene.
responsibilities: Examine patients’ teeth, gums, and oral tissues
Diagnose dental conditions using X-rays and clinical tests
Perform procedures such as fillings, extractions, crowns, and bridges
Conduct root canal treatments
Provide preventive care such as cleanings and fluoride treatments
Educate patients about oral hygiene and dental health
Maintain patient records and treatment plans
Refer patients to dental specialists when needed
required_qualifications: Dental license from dental regulatory authority

roles.title: Family Dentist
roles.overview: A Family Dentist provides dental care for patients of all age groups, from children to elderly individuals. They focus on long-term oral health management for families.
responsibilities: Provide routine dental checkups for all family members
Diagnose and treat common dental problems
Provide pediatric dental care
Manage dental issues related to aging such as tooth loss
Provide preventive treatments like sealants and fluoride
Maintain long-term patient relationships
required_qualifications: Experience in treating patients across age groups

roles.title: Cosmetic Dentist
roles.overview: A Cosmetic Dentist specializes in improving the appearance of teeth, gums, and smiles through aesthetic dental procedures.
responsibilities: Perform teeth whitening treatments
Design and apply dental veneers
Perform smile makeovers
Provide dental bonding and contouring
Correct minor alignment issues
Replace missing teeth with implants or crowns
Develop personalized cosmetic treatment plans

roles.title: Restorative Dentist
roles.overview: A Restorative Dentist focuses on repairing damaged or missing teeth to restore normal function and appearance.
responsibilities: Treat cavities and tooth damage
Perform dental fillings, crowns, and bridges
Restore fractured teeth
Replace missing teeth with implants or dentures
Repair worn or damaged restorations
Work with dental laboratories for prosthetics

roles.title: Preventive Dentist
roles.overview: A Preventive Dentist focuses on preventing dental diseases and promoting oral health through early detection, education, and preventive treatments.
responsibilities: Conduct routine oral examinations
Identify early signs of tooth decay and gum disease
Provide professional cleaning and fluoride treatments
Apply dental sealants
Educate patients on oral hygiene practices
Develop preventive dental care programs

roles.title: Emergency Dentist
roles.overview: An Emergency Dentist provides urgent dental care for patients experiencing severe pain, trauma, infections, or dental injuries.
responsibilities: Treat dental trauma and broken teeth
Manage severe tooth pain and infections
Perform emergency extractions
Treat abscesses and swelling
Provide immediate pain relief
Stabilize dental injuries before specialist referral

roles.title: Hospital Dentist
roles.overview: A Hospital Dentist provides dental care in hospital settings and collaborates with medical teams to manage complex oral health conditions.
responsibilities: Treat patients with complex dental conditions
Manage dental infections affecting overall health
Provide dental care to hospitalized patients
Assist in oral surgeries
Collaborate with surgeons and physicians
Manage dental trauma cases

roles.title: Consultant Dentist
roles.overview: A Consultant Dentist is a highly experienced dental professional who provides expert advice, advanced treatments, and clinical guidance to other dentists or healthcare institutions.
responsibilities: Provide expert dental consultations
Develop complex treatment plans
Supervise junior dentists
Perform advanced dental procedures
Participate in multidisciplinary case discussions
Train dental staff

roles.title: Private Practice Dentist
roles.overview: A Private Practice Dentist owns or operates an independent dental clinic and provides comprehensive dental services to patients.
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat dental conditions
Perform general and preventive dental procedures
Manage dental clinic operations
Hire and supervise dental staff
Maintain patient records and appointments
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations

roles.title: Community Dentist
roles.overview: A Community Dentist focuses on improving oral health in communities through preventive care, education, and outreach programs.
responsibilities: Conduct community dental health programs
Provide dental screenings in schools and rural areas
Educate the public on oral hygiene
Implement preventive dental programs
Treat basic dental conditions in underserved populations
Collaborate with public health organizations
required_qualifications: Training in public health dentistry preferred

roles.title: Orthodontist
roles.overview: An Orthodontist specializes in diagnosing, preventing, and correcting misaligned teeth and jaw irregularities. They use orthodontic appliances such as braces, clear aligners, and retainers to improve dental alignment and bite function.
responsibilities: Diagnose teeth and jaw alignment problems
Design orthodontic treatment plans
Install and adjust braces and aligners
Monitor progress of orthodontic treatments
Correct bite problems such as overbite and underbite
Provide retainers after treatment completion
Educate patients about orthodontic care

roles.title: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon
roles.overview: An Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon performs surgical procedures involving the mouth, jaws, face, and neck. They manage complex dental extractions, facial trauma, oral cancers, and reconstructive surgeries.
responsibilities: Perform complex tooth extractions
Conduct jaw surgeries and facial reconstruction
Treat facial trauma and fractures
Perform dental implant surgeries
Diagnose and treat oral tumors and cysts
Manage cleft lip and palate conditions
Administer surgical anesthesia when required
required_qualifications: MDS in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

roles.title: Endodontist
roles.overview: An Endodontist specializes in diagnosing and treating diseases of the dental pulp and tissues surrounding the roots of teeth. They primarily perform root canal treatments and manage dental pain related to nerve infections.
responsibilities: Diagnose pulp infections and dental pain
Perform root canal treatments
Treat cracked or injured teeth
Perform endodontic retreatment procedures
Manage dental trauma affecting tooth pulp
Use advanced imaging for diagnosis

roles.title: Periodontist
roles.overview: A Periodontist specializes in diagnosing and treating gum diseases and conditions affecting the supporting structures of teeth.
responsibilities: Diagnose gum diseases such as gingivitis and periodontitis
Perform scaling and root planing
Conduct gum surgeries
Treat gum recession
Perform bone grafting procedures
Place dental implants
Manage periodontal infections

roles.title: Prosthodontist
roles.overview: A Prosthodontist specializes in restoring and replacing missing teeth using dental prosthetics such as crowns, bridges, dentures, and implants.
responsibilities: Design and fit dental crowns and bridges
Provide complete and partial dentures
Restore dental implants
Rehabilitate patients with missing teeth
Manage complex restorative cases
Work with dental laboratories for prosthetic design

roles.title: Pediatric Dentist / Pedodontist
roles.overview: A Pediatric Dentist specializes in providing dental care for infants, children, and adolescents, including those with special healthcare needs.
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat dental issues in children
Perform preventive treatments such as fluoride and sealants
Manage early childhood cavities
Monitor dental growth and development
Educate parents about children's oral hygiene
Manage dental anxiety in children

roles.title: Oral Pathologist
roles.overview: An Oral Pathologist specializes in diagnosing diseases affecting the oral cavity, jaws, and surrounding tissues through laboratory analysis and microscopic examination.
responsibilities: Analyze tissue samples and biopsies
Diagnose oral cancers and lesions
Study oral infections and systemic diseases affecting the mouth
Conduct laboratory research
Provide diagnostic reports to dentists and surgeons
Collaborate with medical pathologists

roles.title: Oral Radiologist
roles.overview: An Oral Radiologist specializes in interpreting dental imaging such as X-rays, CT scans, and other diagnostic imaging techniques related to oral and maxillofacial structures.
responsibilities: Interpret dental radiographs and scans
Diagnose oral diseases through imaging
Perform cone beam CT scans
Provide imaging reports for dentists and surgeons
Ensure radiation safety protocols
Maintain imaging equipment
required_qualifications: MDS in Oral Medicine and Radiology

roles.title: Public Health Dentist
roles.overview: A Public Health Dentist focuses on improving oral health at the community and population level through preventive programs, education, and public health initiatives.
responsibilities: Develop community oral health programs
Conduct epidemiological studies on dental diseases
Provide dental screenings in communities
Promote oral hygiene awareness
Implement preventive dental care programs
Collaborate with government health agencies
required_qualifications: Training in epidemiology or community dentistry

roles.title: Oral Medicine Specialist
roles.overview: An Oral Medicine Specialist diagnoses and manages complex oral diseases that may be linked to systemic health conditions.
responsibilities: Diagnose oral mucosal diseases
Treat chronic oral pain disorders
Manage conditions such as oral ulcers and infections
Evaluate oral manifestations of systemic diseases
Coordinate treatment with medical specialists
Provide non-surgical oral disease management

roles.title: Implantologist
roles.overview: An Implantologist specializes in the placement and restoration of dental implants to replace missing teeth. They restore oral function and aesthetics by surgically inserting artificial tooth roots into the jawbone.
responsibilities: Evaluate patients for dental implant suitability
Perform dental implant placement surgeries
Conduct bone grafting procedures when necessary
Restore implants with crowns, bridges, or dentures
Monitor implant healing and osseointegration
Manage complications such as implant failure or infection
Educate patients on implant care and maintenance
required_qualifications: Certification or training in implant dentistry

roles.title: Maxillofacial Trauma Surgeon
roles.overview: A Maxillofacial Trauma Surgeon treats injuries affecting the facial bones, jaws, and oral structures. These surgeons handle trauma cases resulting from accidents, sports injuries, or violence.
responsibilities: Diagnose facial fractures and trauma injuries
Perform surgical repair of jaw and facial bone fractures
Manage soft tissue injuries in the oral and facial region
Reconstruct facial structures after trauma
Collaborate with emergency physicians and plastic surgeons
Manage post-surgical recovery and rehabilitation
required_qualifications: MDS in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

roles.title: Cleft Lip and Palate Surgeon
roles.overview: A Cleft Lip and Palate Surgeon specializes in surgical correction of congenital deformities of the lip and palate, helping restore normal function and appearance.
responsibilities: Diagnose cleft lip and palate conditions
Perform reconstructive surgeries to repair defects
Coordinate treatment with orthodontists and speech therapists
Monitor facial growth and development in children
Provide long-term treatment plans
Manage postoperative care and rehabilitation
required_qualifications: MDS in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

roles.title: Oral Cancer Surgeon
roles.overview: An Oral Cancer Surgeon diagnoses and surgically treats cancers affecting the oral cavity, jaw, and surrounding tissues.
responsibilities: Diagnose oral tumors and cancerous lesions
Perform biopsies and cancer staging procedures
Surgically remove tumors and affected tissues
Reconstruct oral structures after tumor removal
Collaborate with oncologists and radiation specialists
Monitor patients for cancer recurrence
required_qualifications: MDS in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery

roles.title: TMJ Specialist
roles.overview: A TMJ Specialist diagnoses and treats disorders affecting the temporomandibular joint (TMJ), which connects the jaw to the skull.
responsibilities: Diagnose TMJ disorders and jaw pain
Treat jaw dysfunction and joint inflammation
Perform TMJ corrective surgeries when required
Provide bite correction treatments
Manage chronic jaw pain conditions
Recommend physical therapy or appliances such as splints
required_qualifications: MDS in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery / Prosthodontics / Oral Medicine

roles.title: Dental Anesthesiologist
roles.overview: A Dental Anesthesiologist specializes in administering anesthesia and sedation for dental procedures, ensuring patient comfort and safety during complex treatments.
responsibilities: Administer local, intravenous, and general anesthesia
Monitor patient vital signs during procedures
Manage anesthesia-related risks and complications
Provide pain management during dental surgeries
Evaluate patient medical history before sedation
Ensure safe recovery after anesthesia
required_qualifications: Certification in anesthesia and sedation techniques

roles.title: Sedation Dentist
roles.overview: A Sedation Dentist specializes in providing dental care to patients with dental anxiety, special needs, or complex procedures by using sedation techniques to ensure a comfortable experience.
responsibilities: Administer oral, inhalation, or IV sedation
Treat anxious or phobic dental patients
Perform dental procedures under sedation
Monitor patient safety during sedation
Manage sedation-related complications
Educate patients about sedation options

roles.title: Dental Lecturer
roles.overview: A Dental Lecturer is responsible for teaching undergraduate dental students theoretical and clinical aspects of dentistry while supporting research and academic development within a dental college.
responsibilities: Deliver lectures on dental science and clinical subjects
Teach dental anatomy, pathology, and clinical techniques
Guide students during practical laboratory sessions
Assist in clinical training of dental students
Evaluate student assignments and examinations
Participate in academic research and publications
Contribute to curriculum development
required_qualifications: MDS preferred in a dental specialty
Academic teaching certification (optional in some institutions)

roles.title: Assistant Professor (Dentistry)
roles.overview: An Assistant Professor in Dentistry teaches dental students, supervises clinical training, and conducts research while supporting departmental academic activities.
responsibilities: Teach undergraduate and postgraduate dental students
Conduct clinical demonstrations and laboratory sessions
Supervise students during patient treatment in teaching clinics
Conduct research and publish academic papers
Participate in curriculum development
Assist senior faculty in academic administration

roles.title: Associate Professor (Dentistry)
roles.overview: An Associate Professor is a senior academic professional responsible for advanced teaching, supervising research, and leading academic activities within a dental department.
responsibilities: Deliver advanced lectures to dental students
Supervise postgraduate students and research projects
Conduct clinical training sessions
Publish research papers in scientific journals
Participate in academic conferences and seminars
Assist in departmental management and policy planning

roles.title: Professor of Dentistry
roles.overview: A Professor of Dentistry is a senior academic leader responsible for teaching advanced dental subjects, supervising research programs, and guiding academic policies within a dental department.
responsibilities: Deliver specialized lectures to dental students
Lead academic and research initiatives
Supervise postgraduate and doctoral research
Publish research in international journals
Mentor junior faculty members
Represent the department in academic conferences

roles.title: Dean of Dental College
roles.overview: The Dean of a Dental College is the highest academic authority responsible for managing the academic, administrative, and clinical operations of the dental institution.
responsibilities: Oversee academic programs and curriculum development
Manage faculty recruitment and performance evaluation
Ensure compliance with dental education regulations
Develop institutional policies and strategic plans
Supervise research activities and funding initiatives
Represent the institution in academic and professional bodies

roles.title: Head of Department (Dental Specialty)
roles.overview: The Head of Department (HOD) manages a specific dental specialty department such as orthodontics, oral surgery, or prosthodontics in a dental college.
responsibilities: Manage departmental academic programs
Supervise faculty and teaching activities
Coordinate clinical training for students
Oversee research projects within the department
Manage departmental budgets and resources
Ensure quality education and clinical services
required_qualifications: MDS in the respective dental specialty

roles.title: Clinical Instructor (Dentistry)
roles.overview: A Clinical Instructor trains dental students in practical clinical skills, supervising patient treatments and guiding students in real clinical environments.
responsibilities: Train students in clinical dental procedures
Supervise patient treatment performed by students
Provide feedback on clinical performance
Demonstrate proper dental techniques
Ensure adherence to clinical safety protocols
Assist in clinical examinations and evaluations

roles.title: Dental Training Program Director
roles.overview: A Dental Training Program Director manages and oversees dental residency or postgraduate training programs within academic institutions or hospitals.
responsibilities: Design and manage dental training programs
Supervise resident dentists and postgraduate students
Evaluate trainee performance and competencies
Ensure training meets accreditation standards
Coordinate with faculty and hospital administration
Develop educational resources and training modules

roles.title: Dental Research Scientist
roles.overview: A Dental Research Scientist conducts scientific studies to improve dental treatments, oral health technologies, and preventive care methods. They work in research institutions, universities, or dental product companies.
responsibilities: Design and conduct dental research studies
Investigate causes and treatments of oral diseases
Analyze clinical and laboratory research data
Develop innovative dental treatment methods
Publish research findings in scientific journals
Collaborate with dentists, scientists, and healthcare professionals
required_qualifications: MDS or PhD in dental sciences or related field
Research training in clinical or laboratory science

roles.title: Clinical Research Dentist
roles.overview: A Clinical Research Dentist conducts clinical trials to evaluate new dental treatments, drugs, materials, and technologies before they are introduced into clinical practice.
responsibilities: Design and conduct clinical trials for dental treatments
Recruit and monitor patients participating in studies
Ensure compliance with clinical research regulations
Collect and analyze clinical data
Prepare research reports and publications
Collaborate with pharmaceutical and dental product companies
required_qualifications: Training in clinical trial protocols and ethics

roles.title: Oral Biology Researcher
roles.overview: An Oral Biology Researcher studies the biological and molecular mechanisms of oral tissues, teeth, saliva, and oral microorganisms to better understand oral diseases.
responsibilities: Study oral tissue biology and physiology
Investigate oral microbiology and infections
Conduct laboratory experiments related to oral diseases
Develop new treatments for dental conditions
Publish research in scientific journals
Collaborate with biomedical researchers
required_qualifications: PhD in Oral Biology or related biomedical field

roles.title: Dental Materials Researcher
roles.overview: A Dental Materials Researcher studies and develops materials used in dental treatments such as fillings, crowns, implants, adhesives, and prosthetics.
responsibilities: Develop and test dental materials
Evaluate durability and biocompatibility of materials
Conduct laboratory testing and simulations
Collaborate with dental product manufacturers
Improve performance of restorative dental materials
Publish research findings and technical reports
required_qualifications: MDS or PhD in Dental Materials / Biomaterials

roles.title: Dental Epidemiologist
roles.overview: A Dental Epidemiologist studies patterns, causes, and prevention of oral diseases in populations to improve public health policies and dental care programs.
responsibilities: Conduct population-based oral health studies
Analyze trends in dental diseases
Identify risk factors for oral health conditions
Develop preventive dental health programs
Support public health policy development
Publish epidemiological research findings
required_qualifications: MDS in Public Health Dentistry or MPH (Master of Public Health)

roles.title: Biomaterials Scientist (Dental Applications)
roles.overview: A Biomaterials Scientist develops advanced materials used in dental implants, fillings, crowns, and other restorative dental treatments.
responsibilities: Develop new biomaterials for dental applications
Test materials for safety and durability
Conduct laboratory experiments on implant materials
Collaborate with engineers and dental researchers
Improve biocompatibility of dental implants
Support product innovation in dental technology
required_qualifications: MDS / PhD in Biomaterials or Material Science

roles.title: Dental Public Health Researcher
roles.overview: A Dental Public Health Researcher studies oral health issues at the population level and develops strategies to improve access to dental care and preventive services.
responsibilities: Conduct research on community oral health issues
Evaluate dental healthcare programs
Study access to dental care in different populations
Develop public health policies for oral health
Collaborate with government health agencies
Publish public health research reports
required_qualifications: MDS in Public Health Dentistry or MPH

roles.title: Government Dental Surgeon
roles.overview: A Government Dental Surgeon provides dental care in government hospitals and healthcare facilities. They diagnose and treat oral diseases while supporting public healthcare programs.
responsibilities: Examine and diagnose dental problems
Perform dental procedures such as fillings, extractions, and root canal treatments
Provide preventive dental care and oral health education
Maintain patient medical records
Participate in government health programs
Refer complex cases to specialized dental centers
required_qualifications: Registration with the State Dental Council or Dental Council of India

roles.title: Public Health Dentist
roles.overview: A Public Health Dentist focuses on improving oral health at the population level by implementing preventive programs and promoting oral health awareness in communities.
responsibilities: Plan and implement community dental health programs
Conduct dental screenings in schools and rural areas
Educate communities about oral hygiene practices
Monitor oral disease trends in populations
Support national public health initiatives
Collaborate with healthcare professionals and government agencies

roles.title: District Dental Officer
roles.overview: A District Dental Officer supervises and manages dental health services within a district, ensuring effective implementation of government oral health programs.
responsibilities: Supervise dental services across district healthcare facilities
Implement government dental health policies
Manage dental staff in district hospitals and clinics
Monitor oral health programs and outreach initiatives
Prepare reports on district oral health status
Coordinate training programs for dental healthcare workers

roles.title: Community Health Center Dentist
roles.overview: A Community Health Center Dentist provides essential dental services in community healthcare facilities, focusing on preventive and basic restorative dental care.
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat common dental conditions
Provide preventive treatments such as cleanings and fluoride applications
Conduct dental awareness programs in communities
Manage dental emergencies in community health centers
Refer complex cases to district hospitals

roles.title: Primary Health Centre Dentist
roles.overview: A Primary Health Centre (PHC) Dentist provides basic dental care services in rural and semi- urban areas as part of primary healthcare delivery.
responsibilities: Perform routine dental examinations
Treat common dental problems such as cavities and gum disease
Conduct preventive dental programs
Educate patients on oral hygiene and nutrition
Participate in public health campaigns

roles.title: Armed Forces Dental Officer
roles.overview: An Armed Forces Dental Officer provides dental care to military personnel and their families within the healthcare system of the armed forces.
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat dental problems in military personnel
Perform dental surgeries and restorative procedures
Conduct dental health assessments for service members
Participate in military medical operations
Maintain dental readiness for armed forces personnel
required_qualifications: Qualification through recruitment under Indian Armed Forces medical services

roles.title: Railway Dental Officer
roles.overview: A Railway Dental Officer provides dental care to employees of the national railway system and their families.
responsibilities: Provide routine and emergency dental treatments
Conduct dental check-ups for railway staff
Manage dental clinics within railway hospitals
Promote oral health awareness among railway employees
Maintain dental patient records
required_qualifications: Government recruitment through Indian Railways medical services

roles.title: ESI Dental Officer
roles.overview: An ESI Dental Officer provides dental healthcare services to insured workers and their families under the government’s employee health insurance program.
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat dental diseases
Provide dental procedures such as fillings and extractions
Maintain patient medical records
Promote oral health awareness among workers
Participate in preventive dental care programs
required_qualifications: Recruitment through Employees' State Insurance Corporation

roles.title: CGHS Dental Officer
roles.overview: A CGHS Dental Officer provides dental care services to central government employees, pensioners, and their dependents under the government healthcare scheme.
responsibilities: Provide dental diagnosis and treatment
Perform preventive and restorative dental procedures
Maintain dental records for CGHS beneficiaries
Conduct oral health education programs
Refer complex cases to specialized hospitals
required_qualifications: Recruitment through Central Government Health Scheme

roles.title: Medical Advisor (Dental Products)
roles.overview: A Medical Advisor provides scientific and clinical expertise to dental product companies. They support product development, marketing strategies, and clinical research to ensure dental products are safe and effective.
responsibilities: Provide clinical guidance on dental product development
Evaluate scientific evidence for dental products
Support marketing teams with medical and scientific insights
Conduct training sessions for healthcare professionals
Participate in clinical trials and research studies
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations
required_qualifications: MDS preferred in a dental specialty
Experience in clinical dentistry or research

roles.title: Dental Product Specialist
roles.overview: A Dental Product Specialist provides technical knowledge and support for dental products, helping dentists understand how to use new materials, tools, and equipment effectively.
responsibilities: Demonstrate dental products to dentists and clinics
Provide technical support for product usage
Train dental professionals on new dental technologies
Assist sales teams with product knowledge
Gather feedback from dentists to improve products
Participate in dental conferences and exhibitions
required_qualifications: Knowledge of dental materials and technologies

roles.title: Clinical Application Specialist (Dental)
roles.overview: A Clinical Application Specialist trains dental professionals on the proper use of advanced dental technologies and equipment.
responsibilities: Train dentists on new dental equipment and software
Provide clinical support during equipment installation
Demonstrate product features and benefits
Assist dentists with troubleshooting technical issues
Develop educational materials for dental technologies
Collaborate with product development teams
required_qualifications: Experience with dental technology and clinical procedures

roles.title: Dental Equipment Trainer
roles.overview: A Dental Equipment Trainer educates dentists and dental staff on the proper use, maintenance, and safety of dental equipment and instruments.
responsibilities: Train dental professionals on dental equipment usage
Conduct workshops and training sessions
Develop instructional materials and manuals
Demonstrate equipment installation and maintenance
Ensure compliance with safety standards
Provide ongoing technical support
required_qualifications: Knowledge of dental equipment and technology

roles.title: Dental Sales Consultant
roles.overview: A Dental Sales Consultant promotes and sells dental products, equipment, and materials to dental clinics, hospitals, and healthcare institutions.
responsibilities: Promote dental products to dentists and clinics
Build relationships with dental professionals
Provide product demonstrations and presentations
Meet sales targets and business goals
Analyze market trends in the dental industry
Provide after-sales support and customer service

roles.title: Dental Technology Consultant
roles.overview: A Dental Technology Consultant advises dental clinics and organizations on adopting modern technologies such as digital dentistry, CAD/CAM systems, and 3D dental imaging.
responsibilities: Advise clinics on dental technology implementation
Evaluate digital dentistry systems and software
Assist clinics in integrating new technologies
Provide training on digital dental tools
Conduct technology assessments for dental practices
Support digital transformation in dental clinics
required_qualifications: Knowledge of digital dentistry and dental technologies

roles.title: Dental Product Development Manager
roles.overview: A Dental Product Development Manager leads the development of new dental products such as implants, restorative materials, dental equipment, and oral care solutions.
responsibilities: Manage dental product development projects
Collaborate with researchers and engineers
Conduct product testing and validation
Analyze market needs and dental trends
Ensure regulatory compliance for new products
Coordinate product launch strategies
required_qualifications: Experience in product development or research

roles.title: Dental Clinic Owner
roles.overview: A Dental Clinic Owner operates and manages a private dental clinic, overseeing both clinical services and business operations. They ensure high-quality dental care while maintaining financial sustainability and regulatory compliance.
responsibilities: Provide dental treatments and supervise clinical services
Manage daily operations of the dental clinic
Hire and supervise dentists, hygienists, and support staff
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations
Manage clinic finances, budgeting, and revenue
Maintain patient satisfaction and service quality
Implement marketing strategies to grow the clinic
required_qualifications: Dental license from the Dental Council of India or State Dental Council

roles.title: Dental Practice Manager
roles.overview: A Dental Practice Manager oversees the administrative and operational activities of a dental clinic or dental practice to ensure efficient patient care and smooth business operations.
responsibilities: Manage daily clinic operations and staff schedules
Coordinate patient appointments and billing systems
Maintain clinic records and documentation
Monitor clinic performance and financial outcomes
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations
Manage patient satisfaction and service quality

roles.title: Dental Hospital Administrator
roles.overview: A Dental Hospital Administrator manages the operational and administrative functions of a dental hospital, ensuring efficient healthcare delivery and regulatory compliance.
responsibilities: Manage hospital operations and dental services
Supervise dental and administrative staff
Coordinate patient services and hospital workflows
Manage budgets and financial planning
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations
Implement quality improvement initiatives
required_qualifications: BDS or healthcare administration degree (MHA/MBA preferred)

roles.title: Director of Dental Services
roles.overview: The Director of Dental Services oversees dental programs and services across healthcare organizations, hospitals, or large dental networks.
responsibilities: Develop policies for dental healthcare services
Supervise dental staff and specialists
Manage dental program budgets and resources
Ensure quality and safety standards in dental care
Coordinate dental services across multiple facilities
Represent dental departments in healthcare leadership meetings
required_qualifications: Extensive experience in clinical dentistry and healthcare management

roles.title: Chief Dental Officer
roles.overview: A Chief Dental Officer (CDO) is the senior-most dental executive responsible for overseeing dental healthcare services within large healthcare organizations or government health systems.
responsibilities: Develop strategic plans for dental services
Lead dental teams across healthcare facilities
Ensure high standards of dental care and patient safety
Manage budgets and healthcare resources
Advise healthcare leadership on dental policies
Represent the organization in professional healthcare forums

roles.title: Healthcare Operations Manager (Dental Services)
roles.overview: A Healthcare Operations Manager oversees the operational performance of dental clinics or dental departments within hospitals, ensuring efficient patient services and operational effectiveness.
responsibilities: Manage operational workflows in dental clinics
Monitor performance metrics and service quality
Optimize staffing and resource utilization
Implement healthcare management systems
Improve patient service processes
Coordinate with clinical and administrative teams
required_qualifications: BDS or healthcare management degree (MBA/MHA preferred)

roles.title: Cruise Ship Dentist
roles.overview: A Cruise Ship Dentist provides dental care to passengers and crew members aboard cruise ships. They manage dental emergencies, routine treatments, and oral health consultations during voyages.
responsibilities: Diagnose and treat dental conditions for passengers and crew
Manage dental emergencies such as severe tooth pain or trauma
Perform procedures like fillings, extractions, and temporary restorations
Maintain dental equipment and supplies onboard
Educate patients about oral health during travel
Coordinate with ship medical staff for patient care
required_qualifications: Basic life support or emergency medical training preferred

roles.title: Sports Team Dentist
roles.overview: A Sports Team Dentist provides dental care for professional athletes, focusing on oral injuries, preventive dental protection, and dental health related to sports performance.
responsibilities: Treat sports-related dental injuries
Design and provide custom mouthguards
Provide emergency dental treatment during sporting events
Conduct oral health assessments for athletes
Educate athletes about dental injury prevention
Collaborate with sports medicine teams
required_qualifications: Training or experience in sports dentistry preferred

roles.title: Aviation Medical Examiner (Dental Background)
roles.overview: An Aviation Medical Examiner with a dental background evaluates the oral health and medical fitness of pilots and aviation personnel as part of aviation health assessments.
responsibilities: Conduct medical and dental examinations for aviation personnel
Assess oral health conditions that may affect flight safety
Evaluate dental pain risks during high-altitude flights
Provide medical fitness certification reports
Advise pilots on oral health maintenance
required_qualifications: Certification in aviation medicine (required for official examiner roles)
Medical examiner authorization from aviation authorities

roles.title: Humanitarian Dentist
roles.overview: A Humanitarian Dentist provides dental care to underserved populations in low-resource settings, often working with global health organizations and non-profit missions.
responsibilities: Provide dental treatments in underserved communities
Conduct oral health screening programs
Educate communities about oral hygiene
Participate in international health missions
Train local healthcare workers in basic dental care
Support public health initiatives in developing regions
required_qualifications: Experience in community or public health dentistry preferred

roles.title: Disaster Relief Dentist
roles.overview: A Disaster Relief Dentist provides emergency dental care during natural disasters, humanitarian crises, and emergency medical missions.
responsibilities: Provide urgent dental care to disaster victims
Manage dental infections and trauma cases
Assist emergency medical teams in relief operations
Conduct dental triage in emergency settings
Support healthcare infrastructure during crises
required_qualifications: Emergency healthcare or disaster response training preferred

roles.title: Medical Tourism Dentist
roles.overview: A Medical Tourism Dentist provides dental treatments for international patients traveling to other countries for affordable or specialized dental procedures.
responsibilities: Provide advanced dental procedures for international patients
Coordinate treatment plans for visiting patients
Communicate treatment options in international settings
Work with medical tourism facilitators and agencies
Ensure high-quality dental services and patient satisfaction
Provide follow-up care and remote consultation
required_qualifications: Strong communication skills for international patients
work_settings: Private dental centers catering to international patients

roles.title: Locum Dentist
roles.overview: A Locum Dentist works temporarily in dental clinics or hospitals to replace dentists who are absent due to leave, illness, or staffing shortages.
responsibilities: Provide temporary dental care in clinics or hospitals
Diagnose and treat common dental problems
Maintain patient records and treatment plans
Adapt to different clinic environments
Provide continuity of care for patients
required_qualifications: Dental license and registration with the Dental Council of India or relevant international authority
key_skills: Ability to work in different healthcare environments

roles.title: Dental Medico-Legal Consultant
roles.overview: A Dental Medico-Legal Consultant provides expert advice on dental cases that involve legal disputes, malpractice claims, or insurance matters. They analyze clinical records and help lawyers and healthcare organizations understand dental procedures and standards of care.
responsibilities: Review dental records and treatment documentation
Evaluate potential dental malpractice claims
Provide expert opinions on dental treatment standards
Assist attorneys in understanding dental procedures
Prepare medico-legal reports for court cases
Participate in legal consultations and investigations
required_qualifications: Knowledge of healthcare law and medical ethics

roles.title: Forensic Odontologist
roles.overview: A Forensic Odontologist applies dental science to legal investigations, particularly in identifying individuals through dental records and analyzing bite marks in criminal cases.
responsibilities: Identify human remains using dental records
Analyze bite mark evidence in criminal investigations
Assist law enforcement in forensic identification
Participate in disaster victim identification teams
Prepare forensic dental reports for legal authorities
Testify in court regarding forensic dental findings

roles.title: Dental Insurance Consultant
roles.overview: A Dental Insurance Consultant advises insurance companies and dental providers on dental coverage policies, claims evaluation, and treatment cost analysis.
responsibilities: Evaluate dental treatment plans for insurance coverage
Advise insurance companies on dental procedures and costs
Develop dental insurance policies and coverage guidelines
Provide consultation to dental clinics regarding insurance claims
Review complex insurance cases involving dental treatments
required_qualifications: Experience in dental practice or healthcare administration

roles.title: Expert Witness (Dental Cases)
roles.overview: An Expert Witness in dental cases provides professional testimony in legal proceedings involving dental malpractice, injuries, or disputes related to dental treatments.
responsibilities: Review dental records and case documentation
Provide expert opinions on dental treatment standards
Prepare detailed expert witness reports
Testify in court during legal proceedings
Assist attorneys in understanding dental procedures and outcomes

roles.title: Dental Claims Reviewer
roles.overview: A Dental Claims Reviewer evaluates dental insurance claims to determine whether the procedures performed are medically necessary and covered under the insurance policy.
responsibilities: Review dental insurance claims and documentation
Evaluate treatment plans and procedures
Verify medical necessity and policy compliance
Approve or reject claims based on policy guidelines
Communicate with dentists and insurance providers regarding claims
required_qualifications: Knowledge of dental procedures and insurance regulations

roles.title: Digital Dentistry Specialist
roles.overview: A Digital Dentistry Specialist focuses on integrating advanced digital technologies into dental practice. They use digital imaging, intraoral scanners, and computer-assisted systems to improve diagnosis, treatment planning, and dental restorations.
responsibilities: Use intraoral scanners and digital imaging systems for diagnosis
Implement digital workflows for dental treatments
Plan digital smile design and restorative procedures
Coordinate with dental laboratories using digital files
Train dental staff on digital dentistry systems
Maintain digital dental equipment and software
required_qualifications: Experience with CAD/CAM and digital imaging systems

roles.title: CAD/CAM Dental Designer
roles.overview: A CAD/CAM Dental Designer uses computer-aided design and computer-aided manufacturing technologies to design dental restorations such as crowns, bridges, implants, and dentures.
responsibilities: Design dental prosthetics using CAD software
Convert digital scans into dental restoration models
Collaborate with dentists to finalize prosthetic designs
Ensure precise measurements for dental restorations
Operate CAD/CAM milling machines and digital systems
required_qualifications: BDS, Dental Technology degree, or specialized CAD/CAM training
Knowledge of dental anatomy and prosthodontics

roles.title: 3D Dental Printing Specialist
roles.overview: A 3D Dental Printing Specialist uses additive manufacturing technologies to produce dental models, surgical guides, orthodontic appliances, and dental prosthetics using 3D printers.
responsibilities: Prepare digital models for 3D printing
Operate and maintain dental 3D printers
Produce dental aligners, surgical guides, and models
Ensure quality control of printed dental products
Collaborate with dentists and orthodontists for design accuracy
required_qualifications: BDS, Dental Technology degree, or biomedical engineering background

roles.title: Dental AI Researcher
roles.overview: A Dental AI Researcher develops artificial intelligence solutions for dentistry, including automated diagnostics, dental imaging analysis, and predictive dental healthcare systems.
responsibilities: Develop AI models for dental imaging and diagnostics
Analyze large dental datasets for research
Build machine learning tools for treatment prediction
Collaborate with software engineers and dental professionals
Publish research on AI applications in dentistry
required_qualifications: BDS or MDS with research training
Knowledge of AI, machine learning, or data science
Experience in dental imaging research preferred

roles.title: Teledentistry Consultant
roles.overview: A Teledentistry Consultant provides remote dental consultations using telemedicine platforms. They evaluate patients through digital communication tools and guide treatment decisions remotely.
responsibilities: Conduct remote dental consultations
Review digital dental images and patient records
Provide treatment advice and referrals
Support remote oral health monitoring programs
Assist clinics in implementing teledentistry systems
